text.title: VUI Testing Agentic Workflow and Antipatterns
text.summary: Batch runner workflow, adversarial utterance generation, agent gotchas, and human-in-loop gates for VUI testing.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Use Claude to scaffold a four-layer test plan per intent, then generate adversarial utterance batches with nlpaug (synonym substitution, back-translation, noise injection) and run them through the platform CLI. A second agent ingests the resulting transcripts and computes per-intent accuracy, error-rate, and time-to-complete tables. Human-in-loop reviews escalation paths and recordings flagged as "user frustration" by sentiment scoring.

Utterance generation schema: 50 utterances per intent — 10 clean, 20 noisy (background noise annotation), 10 accented (accent label), 10 adversarial (off-topic, code-switch, partial). Include expected intent and expected slots. Output as JSONL for batch CLI runner.
section.reference: Batch intent-accuracy runner against ask-cli simulate; outputs per-intent accuracy table

antipattern.description: Reporting "100% intent accuracy on test set" as launch-ready without real-world participant testing.
antipattern.reason: Synthetic test utterances correlate poorly with real-world speech distributions. Internal-team test sets over-represent the model's own training distribution. Require at least one device-loop test with external participants before launch.

antipattern.description: Using auto-graded scoring as the sole success criterion without a human review pass.
antipattern.reason: Auto-graded scoring underweights tone, empathy, and dialog naturalness. Use a separate sentiment pass before declaring "pass", and hand 20 random failures to a designer for root-cause categorization.

antipattern.description: PII redaction happening after transcripts are passed to the agent.
antipattern.reason: Voice IP/PII redaction must happen BEFORE transcripts reach any LLM. Bake redaction into the runner as a mandatory pre-step, not a post-step. Audio files often contain names, phone numbers, and account details.

antipattern.description: Simulator success declared as device success without device-loop validation.
antipattern.reason: Endpointing differences between simulator and physical device mean simulator success does not guarantee device success. Require at least one full device-loop test per major release.

rule.statement: Lock LLM voice agent seeds during regression runs. Non-deterministic agents cannot be regression-tested; a "passing run" one day may fail the next with identical inputs.
rule.rationale: LLM voice agents drift across runs due to temperature and sampling variance. Fixed seeds produce comparable runs; even then, transcripts (not audio) are the correct regression artifact.

rule.statement: Track "completion at attempt N" separately from "eventual completion rate". Report both metrics. Treat N=4+ as a failure even if the user ultimately succeeded.
rule.rationale: Users who succeed on the fourth attempt do not return. Attempt-count distribution is the signal that identifies systemic re-prompt failures invisible in flat completion rate.
